page: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server running on the QEMU hypervisor. It is equipped with 3 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of system memory, and 80 GB of local SSD storage. The instance does not include a dedicated GPU or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, providing a resource-dense configuration for basic hosting needs.

Benchmark results show strong single-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng score of 2667.29, placing it in the top 25% of tested instances. However, multi-core performance is average at 7981.45, and the memory subsystem represents a clear bottleneck. The Passmark memory score is in the bottom 10% at 1148.15, with memory bandwidth measuring 37.21 GB/sec for large block reads and 20.78 GB/sec for writes.

Due to the shared CPU architecture and limited memory bandwidth, this server is best suited for lightweight, non-intensive workloads. These include basic web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, small-scale development environments, and simple text processing tasks. It is less suitable for memory-heavy databases or highly parallelized compilation.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 3 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 4.0 GB of system memory, and 80 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under the QEMU hypervisor, this instance delivers strong single-core CPU performance, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 2667.29. However, multi-core execution and memory bandwidth represent primary hardware bottlenecks, with memory read-only large block bandwidth limited to 37.21 GB/sec and a poor Passmark memory score. The shared CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio of 1.3 GB per vCPU make this server a budget-friendly option for workloads with low memory demands. It is best suited for lightweight applications, single-threaded processing tasks, basic web serving, and small-scale development environments.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx21 is an x86_64 virtual server with 3 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 80 GB of local SSD storage, delivering strong single-core performance alongside constrained memory bandwidth.

tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local SSD storage for lightweight, single-threaded workloads.
